_comment: Phase 1 OFAT sensitivity sweeps. One factor at a time: sweep each param across N values while holding all others at default. Output drives Phase 2's Optuna search space (drop params whose sweep moves composite by < noise floor). The 'param_path' field is a dotted JSON Pointer into config_overrides — 'base_decay_rates.semantic' becomes config_overrides={'base_decay_rates': {'semantic': X}}. Default values reflect the current Phase 0a-sdk defaults in CognitiveMemoryConfig (types.py).

sweeps.note: α in score = relevance * R^α. Higher α weights retention more. Phase 0g lti-0004 (α=0.5) hinted at noise-dominated effect; phase 1 measures it across 5 values.
Direct-retrieval stability bump (paper Eq 6). Larger boost → faster core promotion.
Associative-retrieval stability bump (paper Eq 8). Affects how much graph-expanded hits get reinforced.
Categorical. Power-law decay was +4.6pp on LongMemEval per CR-E. LTI-Bench cross-check needed.
Only used when decay_model=power. 1.4427 = 1/ln(2), the calibrated match point. Co-sweep with decay_model=power.
β_c for semantic memories (paper Table 2 default 120d). Headline Phase 0a-sdk feature; Phase 0g lti-0005 didn't move accuracy on the small bench. Phase 1 tests on a wider value range.
β_c for episodic memories (paper Table 2 default 45d).
Distinct sessions required for core promotion. Lower → easier promotion → more core memories floored at 0.6.
Access count required for core promotion.
